prompt:
[JC:SYSTEM SCOPE]
You are the [JC:Systems Agent] performing System Boundary Definition for Sub-Phase 3.1.

GOVERNING CONSTRAINTS (apply without exception):
(none)

Define the [JC:System Boundary] — what will be built vs what will be integrated with.

REQUIRED OUTPUT: A JSON object matching the `system_boundary` schema:
- in_scope: array of capabilities/features that will be built
- out_of_scope: array of capabilities explicitly excluded
- external_systems: array of systems outside the boundary, each with:
  - id, name, purpose, interface_type

Rules:
- in_scope must cover all Functional Requirements
- out_of_scope must match the Intent Statement's out_of_scope list
- Every External System identified must have at least one Interface Contract in Sub-Phase 3.3

CONTEXT:
Intent: Product: Consumer Discovery MVP (Phase 1 Focus) — Implementation focused exclusively on Pillar 1 (Home Real Property Assistant) to prioritize user acquisition, property data ingestion (images, 3D splats), and service provider matching for individual homeowners.
Who it serves: Individual homeowners,Small landlords,Property investors managing one or more residences,Hestami Staff (Coordinators)
Problem: Property owners wasting time finding reliable contractors, comparing inconsistent bids, and managing follow-up/scheduling.
Confirmed assumptions: [{"assumption":"The MVP should prioritize the 'AI Agent' discovery experience over a manual directory.","confirmed_by_record_id":"REC-ASS-001"},{"assumption":"Vendor onboarding is treated as an external process (non-internal platform requirement) for V1.","confirmed_by_record_id":"REC-ASS-002"},{"assumption":"Offline capabilities for homeowners are secondary to the primary web-based experience.","confirmed_by_record_id":"REC-ASS-003"},{"assumption":"The 'Digital Asset' ingestion (3D Gaussian Splats, images) is a prerequisite for the service matching platform must replicate Vantaca's 'System of Action' (Action Items) methodology.","confirmed_by_record_id":"REC-ASS-006"},{"assumption":"The MVP targets 'Production Grade' financial handling sites.","confirmed_by_record_id":"REC-ASS-014"},{"assumption":"The MVP assumes a 'Good-Better-Best' quoting model is needed for estimates.","confirmed_by_record_id":"REC-ASS-015"}]
Confirmed constraints: [{"constraint":"Must use SvelteKit for the frontend portal.","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must use Bun for high-performance API execution.","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must use DBOS versioned workflows for the service call lifecycle.","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must enforce Row-Level Security (RLS) for multi-tenant isolation.","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must use SeaweedFS for media asset storage.","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must strictly follow the defined pillars (Phase 1 only) for feature gating.","type":"business"},{"constraint":"Must use Cerbos for policy-based authorization engine (Board vs. Staff access).","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must support Statutory deadlines (Notice requirements) via Rules Engine.","type":"regulatory"},{"constraint":"Must support Mass Email/SMS (Domain 7) for violation notices and meeting notices.","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must adhere to Vantaca model for CAM workflows.","type":"preferential"},{"constraint":"Must support Reserve Studies and Funding Plans (Domain 11).","type":"regulatory"},{"constraint":"Must use DBOS for durable, versioned workflows (Domain 3).","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must integrate with Stripe/Square for Payment capture (Domain 7).","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must use Traefik as the sole ingress proxy.","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must use FFmpeg for video processing (evidence capture).","type":"technical"},{"constraint":"Must strictly follow the ServiceTitan business domains (Identity, Workforce, Pricebook, etc.).","type":"business"}]
Out of scope: ["Pillar 2 (Financials) implementation for this MVP","Pillar 3 (Accounting) implementation for this MVP","Enterprise Governance MVP features (Phase 3 Focus)","Field Service Engine MVP features (Phase 2 Focus)","Automated Homeowner Association (HOA) Voting modules (Phase 3)","Full offline-first capabilities for Homeowners (Secondary priority)","Legacy Vantaca integration for V1 (Replicate 'System of Action' only)","Advanced AI extraction beyond CC&Rs/Bylaws for V1","Direct Contractor Portal Access for V1","Real-time Bid Aggregation for non-homeowners"]
Functional Requirements: 10 User Stories:
  US-001 [critical]: As a Individual Homeowner, I want I want to upload property images and 3D asset data (Splats), so that so that the AI can analyze my property condition and identify needed services.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-001-1: Upload endpoint returns '200 OK' for files under 10MB within 5 seconds; assets indexed in < 10 seconds post-upload.
    AC-001-2: System rejects files not in .png/.jpg/.gltf formats with a specific error code and displays a user-friendly message.
  US-002 [high]: As a Individual Homeowner, I want I want to use the AI Agent to search for service providers, so that so that I receive relevant contractor matches based on my property data.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-002-1: AI response list displayed on screen within 300ms after query; top 5 providers listed with relevance scores.
    AC-002-2: Filter dropdown limits options to 'Home Real Property' categories only; no options from Pillar 2 (Financials) appear.
  US-003 [high]: As a Individual Homeowner, I want I want to view and compare bid estimates from multiple providers., so that so that I can select the best value quote for my property repairs.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-003-1: Comparison table renders with 'Good-Better-Best' columns; minimum 3 bids must be displayed to render the comparison view.
  US-004 [medium]: As a Individual Homeowner, I want I want AI to extract and display CC&R and Bylaws information, so that so that I am aware of property restrictions and compliance rules for my unit.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-004-1: System generates an 'Extracted Clauses' view containing text with >90% confidence score; highlights relevant sections.
  US-005 [critical]: As a Hestami Staff, I want I want to manage and track service action items across the platform, so that so that I can replicate the 'System of Action' methodology for all property owners.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-005-1: Workflow state transitions recorded in audit log every 100ms; status updates (Pending, In-Progress, Complete) reflect immediately in UI.
  US-006 [high]: As a Individual Homeowner, I want I want to receive alerts about statutory deadlines for my property, so that so that I can comply with legal notice requirements and avoid penalties.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-006-1: Email/SMS notification triggered 30 days before deadline date; user receives confirmation receipt acknowledgment.
    AC-006-2: System saves notification payload to queue when offline; delivery attempts resume upon reconnection within 2 hours.
  US-007 [medium]: As a Hestami Staff, I want I want to send mass email/SMS for meeting notices and violation alerts, so that so that I can efficiently communicate with owners regarding compliance issues.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-007-1: Batch message processing completes within 2 minutes for 1,000 recipients; bounce rates tracked in dashboard.
  US-008 [high]: As a Individual Homeowner, I want I want to use the mobile app to verify data on-site, so that so that I can upload evidence and manage tasks away from a desktop environment.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-008-1: App allows camera capture and text entry without network; data syncs successfully upon reconnection with no data loss.
  US-009 [critical]: As a Hestami Staff, I want I want to access system functions while restricted by policy, so that so that sensitive property data remains isolated between tenants and roles.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-009-1: Unauthorized access attempts (e.g., viewing Tenant B data while logged in as Tenant A user) return '403 Forbidden' within 50ms.
  US-010 [medium]: As a Individual Homeowner, I want I want to process payments for my deposits or service fees, so that so that I can secure a booking or pay for repairs securely.
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-010-1: Payment authorization response received within 5 seconds; receipt generated and emailed within 1 minute of confirmation.
Non-Functional Requirements: 12 NFRs:
  NFR-001 [performance]: The Bun-based API backend must maintain low-latency responses for search and asset queries to ensure the 'AI Agent' discovery experience remains responsive. — threshold: P95 API latency must not exceed 200ms for search queries and asset retrieval endpoints.
  NFR-002 [performance]: Media ingestion endpoints using SeaweedFS must sustain high throughput during concurrent property asset uploads without blocking the AI analysis pipeline. — threshold: System must ingest 5GB of mixed media (images, splats) per minute per instance without queue backlog.
  NFR-003 [reliability]: DBOS versioned workflows must ensure state persistence to guarantee no data loss during long-running service matching or compliance check processes. — threshold: Workflow state checkpoints must occur every 1 second to prevent state loss during network interruptions.
  NFR-004 [security]: Row-Level Security (RLS) enforced via the database must ensure multi-tenant isolation prevents unauthorized data access between individual homeowners and staff. — threshold: Unauthorized cross-tenant access attempts must be logged and return 403 Forbidden within 10ms.
  NFR-005 [security]: All data in transit and at rest must be encrypted to protect sensitive property and financial data handled in 'Production Grade' scenarios. — threshold: TLS 1.3 for all external connections; AES-256 for database and media storage encryption at rest.
  NFR-006 [scalability]: The system must scale horizontally to support increased volume of property owners and concurrent service provider matching. — threshold: Support 1,000 concurrent users per tenant without degradation of core AI Agent functionality.
  NFR-007 [reliability]: Offline-mode data synchronization for mobile apps must ensure no data loss when network connectivity is restored. — threshold: Sync queue must process pending data within 30 minutes of reconnection with 100% data integrity.
  NFR-008 [accessibility]: The SvelteKit frontend portal must be usable by individuals with disabilities including screen reader support and keyboard navigation. — threshold: Conformity to WCAG 2.1 Level AA standard with no critical accessibility violations.
  NFR-009 [maintainability]: The platform must provide comprehensive observability to track the health of DBOS workflows and media storage. — threshold: 90% of critical system events (errors, timeouts) must be captured and logged with unique correlation IDs.
  NFR-010 [security]: Authentication tokens and API keys must rotate automatically to prevent credential compromise. — threshold: Authentication tokens must expire after 15 minutes; rotation must occur without downtime.
  NFR-011 [reliability]: Message delivery for statutory notices must complete within a defined SLA even during partial outages. — threshold: 99.5% of mass email/SMS delivery attempts must complete within 5 minutes of message queue acceptance.
  NFR-012 [maintainability]: The codebase must be structured to support versioned updates of DBOS workflows without breaking existing tenant data. — threshold: Workflow migrations must be backward compatible to support 2 major version releases without data migration downtime.
